observational, cohort, case studies, and meta‑analysis

articles on prostate cancer extracted from the survey were

cases attributable to the increase in prevalence of diabetes

CONCLUSION
Based on the survey of the published data, analysis from this 
review indicates that diabetic individuals have an enhanced 
risk of developing cancer. Yet, whether diabetes lead to 
cancer or cancer causes diabetes is still obscure. Further 
research needs to be undertaken so that the etiology behind 
diabetes and cancer could be explicitly obtained.
Financial support and sponsorship
Nil.

There are no conflicts of interest.

correlation obtained in risk of gastric cancer with diabetes 
in both genders.[34]
Hepatic cancer
There are some intriguing findings from the previous 
literature on the association of diabetes with liver cancer.

        ----------------------------------------
         , Question: describe in detail the link in between diabetes and cancer

llm_response.answer: There is a strong evidence linking cancer with diabetes, with diabetic individuals having an enhanced risk of developing cancer. However, whether diabetes leads to cancer or cancer causes diabetes is still obscure. Several epidemiological studies have found that diabetic patients have an increased risk of different types of cancers, such as liver, biliary tract, pancreas, stomach, colorectum, kidney, bladder, breast, and endometrium. On the other hand, a reduced incidence of prostate cancer has been reported in individuals with diabetes. The correlation between diabetes and cancer risk is still being researched, with some studies reporting diversified results.
